declarations.description:
Captures audio from the user's microphone and exposes normalised
per-band volume levels for driving a visualiser.

Use the static AudioAnalyzer.create factory — the constructor
is intentionally private because setup is async (mic permission + Web
Audio API initialisation).
declarations.name: AudioAnalyzer

members.name: dataArray
members.description:
Pre-allocated buffer reused on every getBands call to avoid
per-frame allocations.

members.name: create

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description:
Optional `MediaDeviceInfo.deviceId` to use a specific
microphone. Omit (or pass `undefined`) to use the system default.
Obtain device IDs with `navigator.mediaDevices.enumerateDevices()`.

members.description:
Requests microphone access and creates a fully initialised `AudioAnalyzer`.

Must be called from a user-gesture handler (e.g. a button click) to
satisfy the browser's autoplay / permissions policy.

members.name: getBands

parameters.name: count
parameters.description:
Number of frequency bands to return. Should match the
`bar-count` attribute on `<audio-visualizer>` (typically 3 or 5).

members.description:
Reads the current frequency spectrum and returns `count` normalised
band volumes, each in the range [0, 1].

Call this inside a `requestAnimationFrame` loop for smooth animation.
The underlying `Float32Array` buffer is reused between calls — do not
hold a reference to the returned array across frames; copy if needed.

**Band selection:** only FFT bins 100–199 are used (the ~2–5 kHz
"presence" range). This matches LiveKit's `loPass=100, hiPass=200`
defaults and gives the most perceptually balanced response to speech.

members.name: destroy
members.description:
Disconnects the microphone stream and closes the `AudioContext`,
releasing all associated resources.

After calling `destroy()` the instance must not be used again.
Create a new `AudioAnalyzer` via AudioAnalyzer.create if
you need to restart.

declarations.description:
`<audio-visualizer>` — a real-time microphone frequency visualiser.

Displays N animated bars whose heights reflect the current volume in
corresponding frequency bands captured from the user's microphone.

## Quick start

```html
<!-- 1. Import (once per page) -->
<script type="module" src="audio-visualizer.js"></script>

<!-- 2. Place the element -->
<audio-visualizer id="viz" size="md" color="#6366f1"></audio-visualizer>

<!-- 3. Start from a user gesture -->
<button onclick="document.getElementById('viz').startMicrophone()">
  Start
</button>
```

## Attributes

| Attribute    | Type            | Default        | Description                          |
|--------------|-----------------|----------------|--------------------------------------|
| `size`       | `VisualizerSize`| `'md'`         | Visual scale of the component        |
| `color`      | CSS color string| `currentColor` | Bar fill colour                      |
| `bar-count`  | `number`        | 3 or 5         | Number of frequency bands to display |

## Methods

| Method                          | Description                                       |
|---------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
| `startMicrophone(deviceId?)`    | Request mic access and start the animation loop   |
| `stopMicrophone()`              | Stop the animation loop and release the mic       |

## CSS custom properties

| Property                              | Default        | Description                                                       |
|---------------------------------------|----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `--audio-visualizer-color`            | `currentColor` | Bar colour. Overrides the `color` attribute and CSS inheritance   |
| `--audio-visualizer-transition`       | `100ms`        | Bar height transition duration                                    |
declarations.name: AudioVisualizer

cssProperties.description: Bar fill colour. Takes priority over the `color` attribute and CSS colour inheritance (`currentColor`). Set via CSS for stylesheet-based theming. Default: unset (falls back to `color` attr or the inherited `currentColor`).
cssProperties.name: --audio-visualizer-color

cssProperties.description: Transition duration for bar height changes. Increase for a smoother, slower response; decrease for snappier reaction to transient sounds. Default: `100ms`.
cssProperties.name: --audio-visualizer-transition

members.name: size
members.description:
Visual size variant.
Controls bar width, container height, and gap between bars.

members.name: color
members.description:
Bar fill colour — any valid CSS color value (`#hex`, `rgb()`, named
colour, etc.). When omitted the bars inherit `color` from the
surrounding document via `currentColor`.

Prefer the `--audio-visualizer-color` CSS custom property for
stylesheet-based theming — it takes priority over this attribute.

members.name: barCount
members.description:
Number of frequency-band bars to display.
Defaults to 3 for `icon`/`sm` sizes and 5 for `md`/`lg`/`xl`.

members.name: bands
members.description: Normalised [0, 1] volume per band, updated every animation frame.

members.name: micState
members.description: Current lifecycle state of the microphone stream.

members.name: analyzer
members.description: Multiband frequency analyzer backed by the Web Audio API.

members.name: rafId
members.description: ID returned by `requestAnimationFrame`, used to cancel the loop.

members.name: isActive
members.description:
`true` while a microphone stream is active and the animation loop is
running. Useful for toggling button labels or disabling UI controls.

members.name: startMicrophone

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description:
Optional `MediaDeviceInfo.deviceId` for a specific
microphone. Obtain IDs via `navigator.mediaDevices.enumerateDevices()`.
Omit to use the system default input device.

members.description:
Requests microphone access and starts the bar animation loop.

- **Must be called from a user gesture** (e.g. a button `click` handler)
  to satisfy the browser's permissions policy.
- If already active, the current stream is stopped and a new one is
  started with the supplied `deviceId` — safe to call on device switch.

members.name: stopMicrophone
members.description:
Stops the animation loop and releases the microphone stream.

Safe to call at any time, including when the mic is not yet active.
The component resets to its idle (dot) state.

members.name: tick
members.description:
`requestAnimationFrame` callback — reads the latest band volumes from
the analyser and triggers a Lit re-render by updating `this.bands`.

Named `tick` rather than `animate` to avoid shadowing
`Element.animate()` from the Web Animations API.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
Visual size variant.
Controls bar width, container height, and gap between bars.

attributes.name: color
attributes.description:
Bar fill colour — any valid CSS color value (`#hex`, `rgb()`, named
colour, etc.). When omitted the bars inherit `color` from the
surrounding document via `currentColor`.

Prefer the `--audio-visualizer-color` CSS custom property for
stylesheet-based theming — it takes priority over this attribute.

attributes.name: bar-count
attributes.description:
Number of frequency-band bars to display.
Defaults to 3 for `icon`/`sm` sizes and 5 for `md`/`lg`/`xl`.
